bodyHtml.zh-CN:
产品改进

成就系统正式切到事件驱动，反馈更贴近真实操作

  • 变更说明: 成就推进不再主要围绕旧的倒计时和重置时钟，而是改成根据真实产品行为触发。这次还补上了产品行为分组、看板页头成就轨道，以及 token 消耗里程碑扩展到 100 万，成长反馈终于和“你刚刚做了什么”直接对上。
  • 影响范围: 影响成就系统、Hagipower 类进度提示、看板页头反馈以及长期使用时的进度感知。
  • 使用方式: 按原来的会话、看板和项目流程继续使用即可，新的事件统计和里程碑会自动生效。
  • 示例: 在看板里推进任务、持续消耗 token 完成实际工作后，会比旧版本更容易看到对应的进度反馈。
  • 注意事项: 旧的倒计时式交互已经退出主路径，后续成就反馈会更偏向“完成行为”而不是“维持计时”。

标准短语从临时输入技巧升级为正式能力

  • 变更说明: 标准短语现在同时具备后端配置服务、前端管理界面，以及和路径引用、提及流程的联动，不再只是临时填一句常用文本的小功能，而是可长期维护的复用文案方案。
  • 影响范围: 影响设置与配置、标准短语管理、消息输入以及提及流程。
  • 使用方式: 在标准短语管理入口整理团队或个人常用语句，然后从支持的输入场景直接插入。
  • 示例: 需要反复发相同的执行说明、审查要求或命令片段时，可以预先保存成短语，再在输入框中快速插入。
  • 注意事项: 同版本已经移除旧的 quick prompts；如果之前依赖那套入口，建议把常用内容迁移到标准短语。

路径引用终于成为一等输入能力

  • 变更说明: 这版把路径引用从零散辅助功能补齐成完整能力，包括独立服务、候选 API、token 系统、前端状态、选择器接入和消息归一化。文件路径、目录路径、项目上下文可以更稳定地被系统接住，而不是靠手工粘贴一长串文本。
  • 影响范围: 影响消息输入、路径选择、会话消息规范化、客户端状态处理以及后续任务上下文传递。
  • 使用方式: 在支持的输入框中继续使用路径引用选择器或相关输入方式，系统会负责把路径解析、token 化并归一化。
  • 示例: 在对话里引用某个项目目录或具体文件时，可以直接通过选择器注入上下文，而不是自己反复手打路径。
  • 注意事项: 这组能力前后端都有改动，所以在“对话里频繁传递工程上下文”的场景里提升最明显。

Git 提交和研究调查流程更容易走到执行阶段

  • 变更说明: Git 面板补上了 AI Commit 生成功能和仓库列表手动刷新，研究调查流程则新增项目引用选择器，并把目标项目写进 prompt 与 autotask 流程里。结果是从“先调查一下”切到“开始落地执行”之间的断层少了很多。
  • 影响范围: 影响 Git 工具、仓库管理、研究/调查任务配置，以及项目详情到看板的衔接。
  • 使用方式: 继续使用现有的 Git 和研究入口即可，新增动作直接出现在原来的流程中。
  • 示例: 仓库列表状态不新时可以先手动刷新；研究任务需要明确落到某个项目时，可以在开始前先选定目标项目。
  • 注意事项: 这些变化不只是多了几个按钮，背后还包含 prompt 和自动任务链路的配套调整。

高密度工作区终于更好扫了

  • 变更说明: 会话列表、看板泳道、状态栏都补上了更完整的折叠和紧凑控制，吞吐信息区域也做了更适合紧凑布局的处理；再加上主题色工具整理和夏季主题支持，长时间盯着密集界面时没那么乱。
  • 影响范围: 影响会话列表、看板视图、状态栏、主题展示以及高密度工作区的可读性。
  • 使用方式: 在你原本会调折叠、紧凑或主题的入口继续使用即可，这次不需要学新的控制方式。
  • 示例: 当会话列表过长或看板泳道过多时，可以切到更紧凑的展示，保持屏幕上信息量但少一点视觉噪声。
  • 注意事项: 这批调整还顺手统一了部分组件样式，所以密集模式下的交互一致性也比之前更稳。

会话头像区和模型信息更清楚了

  • 变更说明: 会话 hero 区开始展示 profession metadata，兼容的次模型会被尽量保留，默认模型配置也同步刷新。对经常切换角色、模型或会话身份的人来说，这版把“当前到底在用什么配置”说明白了一些。
  • 影响范围: 影响会话顶部信息、角色/职业快照、模型兼容展示以及执行快照中的语言偏好处理。
  • 使用方式: 打开现有会话时，留意 hero 区和相关模型信息即可，不需要额外设置。
  • 示例: 当一个角色带有职业快照或兼容的次模型时，界面现在更容易把这些信息完整带出来，而不是悄悄丢掉一部分上下文。
  • 注意事项: 语言偏好的处理也被移到了更贴近执行快照的位置，重点是减少复杂执行流里的配置漂移。

词云和数据聚合不再只看“有没有”，开始看“最近发生了什么”

  • 变更说明: 词云关键词排名改成按时间权重计算，数据聚合同时增加了强制刷新支持。相比只堆历史量，这更适合追踪最近一段时间里真正升温的话题和活动。
  • 影响范围: 影响词云排序、数据聚合结果和需要即时刷新的统计视图。
  • 使用方式: 继续使用现有词云和相关统计视图即可；当你需要确认最新数据时，可以触发更直接的刷新流程。
  • 示例: 最近几轮会话突然频繁出现的关键词，会比旧版本更容易排到前面，而不是被更早但更久的历史数据压住。
  • 注意事项: 这项变化偏分析体验提升，不一定对应单独的新页面，但对看趋势和复查近期重点更有帮助。

修复

Orleans 会话操作回到了正确的数据边界

  • 变更说明: Orleans 驱动的会话操作不再错误参与不合适的 UnitOfWork 边界，修掉了一类会让会话执行、持久化或复杂链路表现发飘的底层问题。
  • 影响范围: 影响会话执行、会话持久化和服务端流程稳定性。
  • 使用方式: 不需要额外操作，升级后原有会话流程会自动受益。
  • 注意事项: 这属于正确性修复，平时你可能只会感觉它“更稳了”，但在复杂任务里价值很大。

项目到仓库的解析路径更直接

  • 变更说明: 后端把项目仓库查找改成了更直接的 ORM 查询路径，少绕了一层，仓库上下文解析更可预测。
  • 影响范围: 影响项目仓库解析、依赖仓库上下文的 Git 和路径引用操作，以及相关后端查询性能。
  • 使用方式: 继续照常使用依赖仓库上下文的能力即可。
  • 注意事项: 这一点和本版本的 Git、路径引用增强是连在一起的，因为这些流程都吃仓库上下文的准确度。

Markdown 列表和紧凑布局的细节终于收紧了

  • 变更说明: 会话内容里的 markdown 列表标记对齐问题已修复，多处折叠和紧凑状态下的 UI 细节也做了收口，高密度界面不再像之前那样有些位置松、有些位置挤。
  • 影响范围: 影响会话内容渲染、紧凑布局、折叠状态表现和高密度界面的整体稳定性。
  • 使用方式: 如果你之前在会话内容、侧栏或看板里见过间距别扭、列表对不齐的问题，升级后可以直接回到这些位置复查。
  • 注意事项: 周边提交里虽然带着一些样式和测试调整，但最后能看到的结果就是界面更整齐、更稳定。
bodyHtml.en:
Product Improvements

Achievements now follow real actions instead of timer rituals

  • Change Summary: Achievement progression no longer leans on the older countdown and reset-clock model. It now reacts to concrete product-behavior events, adds grouped behavior tracking, surfaces achievement rails in kanban headers, and extends token-consumption milestones up to one million.
  • Impact: Affects the achievement system, Hagipower-style progress feedback, kanban header signals, and long-session progression visibility.
  • How to Use: Keep using normal session, kanban, and project workflows. The new event tracking and milestone thresholds apply automatically after upgrade.
  • Example: Moving work through kanban or burning through large token volumes now produces progress feedback that lines up more closely with what you actually just did.
  • Notes: Countdown-style interactions were removed from the main path, so progression is now centered on completed actions rather than maintained timers.

Standard phrases become a real reusable-content system

  • Change Summary: Standard phrases now have backend configuration support, a dedicated management UI, and integration with path-reference mention flows. This turns them into a maintainable reusable-content workflow instead of a lightweight prompt shortcut.
  • Impact: Affects settings/configuration, standard phrase management, message composition, and mention flows.
  • How to Use: Open the standard phrase management entry, curate shared or personal phrases there, then insert them from supported input surfaces.
  • Example: Repeated execution instructions, review language, or command snippets can now be saved once and reused directly in the composer.
  • Notes: Legacy quick prompts were removed in the same release. Teams that depended on them should move their reusable text into standard phrases.

Path references are finally a first-class way to pass context

  • Change Summary: This release fills in the entire path-reference stack with dedicated services, candidate APIs, token handling, client-side state, picker integration, and message normalization. File paths, directories, and project context are now much easier to hand off without relying on raw pasted text.
  • Impact: Affects message input, path selection, session message normalization, client-side state, and downstream task context transfer.
  • How to Use: Use the path-reference picker or related input affordances in supported composers. The system now resolves, tokenizes, and normalizes those references more reliably.
  • Example: When you need to point a session at a specific project folder or file, you can inject that context through the picker instead of manually typing long paths.
  • Notes: The improvement spans both backend and frontend changes, so it is most noticeable in workflows that repeatedly pass project context through conversation.

Git and research flows now connect more cleanly to execution

  • Change Summary: Git workflows gained AI commit generation and manual repository-list refresh, while research investigation now supports explicit project targeting through selectors, prompts, and autotask flows. The gap between “investigate” and “do the work” is noticeably smaller.
  • Impact: Affects Git tooling, repository management, research/investigation setup, and the project-detail-to-kanban handoff.
  • How to Use: Keep using the existing Git and research panels. The new actions are embedded in those flows instead of requiring a separate setup path.
  • Example: If repository metadata looks stale, refresh it directly from the Git pane; if a research task should land in a specific project, choose that target before starting.
  • Notes: These changes are broader than a few extra buttons because prompt wiring and automation handoff were updated at the same time.

Dense workspaces are easier to scan for longer stretches

  • Change Summary: Session lists, kanban swimlanes, and the status bar all gained more complete collapsed and compact controls. Throughput UI was adjusted for tighter layouts, and theme utility cleanup plus summer-theme support make busy screens feel less noisy over time.
  • Impact: Affects session lists, kanban views, the status bar, theme presentation, and overall readability in high-density workspaces.
  • How to Use: Use the collapse, compact, and theme controls where you already manage workspace density or appearance. No new learning path is required.
  • Example: If a session list grows too long or a kanban board gets crowded, you can tighten the view without giving up as much on-screen context.
  • Notes: Related component styling was also aligned, so dense modes now behave more consistently instead of feeling pieced together.

Session identity and model context are easier to read

  • Change Summary: Session hero surfaces now include profession metadata, compatible secondary models are preserved more reliably, and default model configuration was refreshed. For users who switch roles, model combinations, or session identities often, the active setup is clearer than before.
  • Impact: Affects session header information, role/profession snapshots, model compatibility display, and execution-snapshot handling for language preference.
  • How to Use: Open existing sessions as usual and check the hero area and related model details. No extra setup is required.
  • Example: If a role carries profession metadata or a compatible secondary model, the UI is less likely to silently drop that context.
  • Notes: Language preference handling was also moved closer to the execution snapshot path to reduce configuration drift in more complex runs.

Word clouds and aggregated views now pay more attention to recency

  • Change Summary: Keyword ranking in the word cloud now uses time-weighted ordering, and data aggregation gained force-refresh support. That makes recent activity easier to spot instead of letting older volume dominate the picture.
  • Impact: Affects word-cloud ordering, aggregation results, and statistics views that need a more immediate refresh path.
  • How to Use: Keep using the existing word-cloud and analytics surfaces; when you need fresher data, use the stronger refresh path behind aggregation.
  • Example: Keywords that surged in the most recent sessions are more likely to rise in the cloud instead of being buried under much older history.
  • Notes: This is more about analysis quality than a brand-new page, but it materially improves short-horizon trend review.

Fixes

Orleans session operations are back inside the right data boundary

  • Change Summary: Orleans-backed session operations no longer participate in the wrong UnitOfWork boundary. This removes a class of low-level reliability problems that could make session execution, persistence, or multi-step flows behave inconsistently.
  • Impact: Affects session execution, session persistence, and server-side workflow stability.
  • How to Use: No extra action is required. Existing session workflows benefit automatically after upgrade.
  • Notes: This is mainly a correctness fix, so most users will feel it as “more stable” rather than see a new capability.

Project-to-repository resolution takes a more direct path

  • Change Summary: Backend repository lookup now uses a more direct ORM query path, cutting out unnecessary indirection and making repository context resolution easier to predict.
  • Impact: Affects project repository resolution, Git and path-reference flows that depend on repository context, and related backend lookup performance.
  • How to Use: Keep using repository-dependent workflows as usual.
  • Notes: This matters more in this release because the Git and path-reference improvements both rely on repository context being accurate.

Markdown lists and compact layouts were tightened up

  • Change Summary: Markdown list-marker alignment in session content was fixed, and several collapsed or compact UI states were cleaned up so dense screens no longer feel uneven from one panel to the next.
  • Impact: Affects session content rendering, compact layouts, collapsed-state behavior, and the overall stability of high-density workspaces.
  • How to Use: Revisit session content, side panels, or kanban layouts where you previously saw uneven spacing or awkward compact behavior.
  • Notes: Some surrounding commits were style or test work, but the visible outcome is a cleaner and steadier interface.
